purpose: Eval fixtures for the planned screenshot-matching feature (classical CV: sprite template matching + OCR; digital screenshots only, photos and LLM explicitly out of scope). See docs/TEAM-SOURCES.md 'Screenshot matching'. Provided by the project owner 2026-07-06.
scopeNote: DECISION 2026-07-06: Showdown screenshots only for now — all Champions (mobile/NS) fixtures are future work (fixtures carry a scope field; the eval skips scope:'future'). Findings recorded for that future work: Champions mobile team screens use Champions' own NEW menu-sprite artwork (matches neither Showdown box icons nor pokesprite gen8 HOME icons; no public source exists yet for a game this new). Champions select screens DO reuse the official HOME renders (= Showdown dex sprites — verified visually), so the doubles-select strip becomes matchable the moment the dex template set is enabled. Showdown surfaces render XY-style animated sprites (sprites/ani/, hyphenated forme filenames) — templates take 3 frames per species. negative-starter-illustration.png must match no template set.
groundTruthNotes: expectedSpecies is authoritative only where the screenshot contains text naming the mons (the Japanese Champions team screens). Sprite-only screenshots carry unverified best-guess candidates ('?' = low confidence) — confirm during the first eval run and then promote to expectedSpecies.

fixtures.description: Cropped Showdown team preview header: format tag [gen9championsvgc2026regmb], player 'YOASOBI stan', 6 minisprites, no species text.
Pokemon Champions mobile team screen (ability/moves tab), Japanese. Species names in text; slot 4 is nicknamed 'Hai' (Garchomp by sprite/ability). Rich ground truth incl. items/abilities/moves.
Same team as champions-mobile-team-abilities-ja.png, stats tab (HP/Atk/... with EV-like allocations). Same expected species; nickname 'Hai' on the Garchomp slot.
Champions doubles ('だぶる') selection strip: 6 model renders with held-item icons, no species text.
Showdown battle team preview (animated battle sprites with gender + L50 tags), no species text.
fixtures.groundTruthSource: parsed from siguii2's replay gen9championsvgc2026regmb-2645089420 via our own parser (authoritative)

fixtures.description: Showdown battle side panel: trainer 'Siguii2' with 6 tiny minisprites (hardest case: ~30px sprites). Appears to be the same/similar team as showdown-battle-preview-models.png.
fixtures.groundTruthSource: parsed from siguii2's replay gen9championsvgc2026regmb-2645089420 via our own parser (authoritative)

fixtures.description: Cute starter-Pokemon illustration (Rowlet/Litten/Popplio/Sprigatito/Fuecoco/Quaxly with name tags). HARD negative: 6 valid, readable species names but NOT a team/battle screen — species-count validation alone would wrongly pass it. The model's layout classification must return recognized:false (artwork, not a competitive team).
Champions battle team preview, vertical layout (opponent 'ネギねこ', Standing By): 6 slots with model renders, type + gender icons only, some models partially obscured/shiny. Hardest Champions case.

negativeCasesTodo: One hard negative on file (Pokemon artwork with valid names). Still add 1-2 fully unrelated screenshots (non-Pokemon game, desktop) before the eval.

evalScorecard.config: precision-first gates + strict-anchor grid completion (side panel 2x3 / preview 1x6 layouts): confident hits reveal the grid, empty slots classify with relaxed gates and are marked inferred ('Name?' in the UI), off-grid hits (trainer sprite) are dropped. Grid anchors must be strict finals — artwork also arranges subjects in grids (the negative is a 2x3 illustration).

results.showdown-side-panel-minisprites.png: PASS 4/6 correct (Basculegion, Raichu, Whimsicott, Incineroar; inferred-wrong: Froslass-Mega for Ninetales-Alola, Cosmog for faded Gholdengo — Gholdengo's sprite is unrecoverable even by hand, corr 0)
results.showdown-battle-preview-models.png: partial: 1/3 correct (Ninetales-Alola; wrong: Finizen, Wimpod) — too few strict anchors to infer the row grid

evalScorecard.verdict: EXPERIMENTAL: the in-battle side panel — the primary real-world surface — extracts 4/6 with wrong fills marked '?'. Crowded team-preview renders stay partial. Root cause stands: grid-signature affine matching under overlap/occlusion; durable fix = embedding-gallery/trained model.
